source_pr.title: *: fix store token is up to the limit in test
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #35375

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Why do we need to change this value?

Because we find that if it is set at a low value, the next test cases will use it again and it will have an effect on the test case. so it will raise the error ```store token is up to the limit```

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to environment/external dependencies (resource contention, ports, or real cluster timing). This PR stabilizes it by: *: fix store token is up to the limit in test.
fix_pattern: *: fix store token is up to the limit in test

evidence: evidence: *: fix the unstable test to not set low value on tidb_store_limit
